headline: Trex Company Adds Lee Coker to Leadership Team as Vice President, Corporate Development and Investor Relations
summary: WINCHESTER, Va., March 02, 2026--Trex Company, Inc. [NYSE:TREX], the world’s largest manufacturer of high-performance, low-maintenance composite decking and railing products, today announced that Lee Coker has joined Trex as Vice President, Corporate Development and Investor Relations.

raw.headline: Trex Company Adds Lee Coker to Leadership Team as Vice President, Corporate Development and Investor Relations
raw.summary: WINCHESTER, Va., March 02, 2026--Trex Company, Inc. [NYSE:TREX], the world’s largest manufacturer of high-performance, low-maintenance composite decking and railing products, today announced that Lee Coker has joined Trex as Vice President, Corporate Development and Investor Relations.

headline: AAB invests in FM Accountants and LSMQ to deepen presence in Ireland
summary: Upon completion of the transactions, both FM Accountants and LSMQ will operate under the AAB name.

headline: 3 Mega-Cap Stocks with Exciting Potential
summary: Megacap stocks are behemoths that set the tone for their industries, and their massive scale typically leads to wide moats. However, the downside is that most have already exploited their existing market opportunities and must invest heavily to expand further, a risky proposition.

raw.headline: 3 Mega-Cap Stocks with Exciting Potential

headline: Coca-Cola Dividend Streak And New IR Chief Underline Valuation Debate
summary: Coca-Cola (NYSE:KO) announced its 64th consecutive annual dividend increase. The company also appointed Todd Beiger as vice president and new head of investor relations. These updates highlight developments in shareholder returns policy and investor communication leadership. Coca-Cola, trading at $81.56, sits in a well followed corner of the market for income focused investors, with its dividend record often viewed as a key part of its appeal. The stock is up 1.2% over the past week, 9.0%...

headline: Berkshire Hathaway's New CEO Omits Bank of America And Chevron In Letter To Shareholders After Warren Buffett's Exit
